3.text:
The biggest problem here is that most agencies--like MMDA--will implement it in a different way.

If an agency opts for a compressed work week (4 days work, 10 hours of per day), isn't that going to result in the same amount of energy consumed as a 5-day, 8-hour work week?

If the objective of the 4 day workweek is to reduce the transportation expenses of employees. I believe such objective  can be attained.

However, by stretching the working hours, productivity is affected. A human comprehension is effective only on the 1st 45 minutes. It plateaus for the rest of the day. In an 8 hour workday, an 80% daily productivity is the ideal number. But hardly consistently achievable. What more of a 10 hour workday.

Productivity has never been about long hours. 

Why not retain a 4 day 8 hour workday and a work from home on the 5th?

The deadline for this problem was decades ago. Our government has always been reactive in addressing problems but proactive in stealing from our Treasury.

During the early 70’s, we have experienced the first global oil shock. Ever since , we have learned that OPEC will weaponize their oil products.

But our leaders have never learned to address the oil problem properly.

The oil deregulation program started on the wrong foot. The new entrants were never given the chance to have a head start to even the playing field. 

Even the weekly oil adjustment that was based on MOPS could not be scrutinized since it is confidential. Thus, there was no transparency.

If my memory serves me right, only Petron has a refinery while everyone imports their oil in finished products.

It is high time to review the oil deregulation law to address its flaws for the benefit of the future generation.

This price hike is just a start, if this conflict further escalates and its escalating everyday, kakaattack lang sa Iran airport - who knows up to what amount the fuel could go up. Fuel prices go up, electricity go up, prices of foods and other goods go up. All the while giving Russia more opportunity to fund its war against Ukraine. And as we have millions of Filipimos in the middle east, who knows up to what damage that could be in the inflow of remittances and of course life/safety of our fellow citizens. Even bringing them all back will be a nightmare, how can we with the sheer volume of them eh kahit developed countries are struggling to evacuate theirs. If there are still Filipinos supporting Trump - shame on you.

Ps. I hope more and more private companies also allow flexible working conditions in light of whats going on.
Suspend na dapat ang excise tax muna sa fuel para naman makaluwag sa mga tao
